Title: Biosynthesis of bioactive diterpenoids in the medicinal plant Vitex agnus-castus

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Vitex agnus-castus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: labdane-type diterpenoid backbone formation; C-9 hydroxylated branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes GGPP to produce peregrinol diphosphate, a C-9 hydroxylated class II diterpene precursor that is detected as peregrinol after dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes relative to whole leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex leaf and fruit trichomes; assayed by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: bioactive labdane/furan/lactone diterpenoids including vitexilactone-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dopaminergic diterpenoids linked to prolactin-lowering pharmaceutical activity and treatment of premenstrual syndrome
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: provides C-9 hydroxylated diterpene backbone precursor for bioactive diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; combinatorial coupling with class I diTPSs; GC-MS; NMR; expression analysis by qRT-PCR; metabolite detection in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Peregrinol was observed even when VacTPS1 was expressed alone, likely due to endogenous phosphatase activity in Nicotiana benthamiana or possible phosphatase side activity by class I partners; second reported class II diTPS yielding a C-9 hydroxylated product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable entry enzyme for reconstructing C-9 hydroxylated diterpenoid pathways and for combinatorial engineering of medicinal diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS1 was identified from trichome transcriptomics as a trichome-enriched class II diTPS. 2. Transient expression produced peregrinol, and coupling with MvELS yielded 9,13-epoxylabd-14-ene, indicating VacTPS1 forms peregrinol diphosphate. 3. VacTPS1 supplies a key precursor for downstream bioactive Vitex diterpenoids and for CYP76BK1-mediated oxidation.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: peregrinol diphosphate; syn-copalyl diphosphate; ent-copalyl diphosphate; (+)-8-labda-13-en-8-ol diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 9,13(R)-epoxy-labd-14-ene and viteagnusin D from peregrinol diphosphate; syn-manool/vitexifolin A from syn-CPP; ent-manool from ent-CPP; 13R-(+)-manoyl oxide from (+)-8-LPP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: dephosphorylates and rearranges multiple class II diterpene diphosphates to generate diverse diterpene olefins/alcohols including viteagnusin D and syn-manool
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase, TPS-e/f, bi-domain enzyme lacking N-terminal gamma domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes relative to whole leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex trichomes; function tested in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: bioactive labdane and related diterpenoids including viteagnusin D and vitexifolin-type products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: diterpenoids in Vitex extracts are implicated in dopaminergic bioactivity and prolactin-lowering medicinal effects
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: generates multiple diterpene scaffolds from different diphosphate precursors, contributing strongly to Vitex diterpenoid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression with class II diTPSs in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR; comparison with characterized reference enzymes; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: substrate promiscuity may create mixed product profiles in engineering contexts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Weak coupling with VacTPS5 was suggested but not definitive; enzyme shows high substrate promiscuity and is highlighted as a useful combinatorial biosynthetic tool
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for synthetic biology to generate diverse high-value diterpene scaffolds; can be paired with multiple class II diTPSs for pathway diversification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS2 is a trichome-enriched class I diTPS from the Lamiaceae specialized metabolism clade. 2. With VacTPS1 it forms 9,13(R)-epoxy-labd-14-ene and viteagnusin D; with VacTPS3 it forms syn-manool/vitexifolin A. 3. VacTPS2 accepts several non-native diterpene diphosphates, demonstrating broad substrate promiscuity and a major role in scaffold diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes GGPP to syn-copalyl diphosphate, which can be converted by downstream class I diTPSs to syn-manool/vitexifolin A and related diterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes relative to whole leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex trichomes; function tested in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: part of medicinal diterpenoid diversity in Vitex species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supplies syn-CPP precursor for downstream formation of syn-manool/vitexifolin A and other syn-labdane diterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; coupling with SsSS; GC-MS; isolation of syn-copalol derivative; NMR/HSQC; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Absolute configuration of the product was not firmly established due to low isolated amounts; alcohol derivative syn-copalol showed weak dextrorotation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful precursor-generating enzyme for engineering syn-labdane diterpenoid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS3 was identified as a trichome-enriched class II diTPS. 2. Product profiles matched rice OsCPSsyn, and coupling with SsSS yielded syn-manool/vitexifolin A. 3. Isolation of syn-copalol confirmed that VacTPS3 is a syn-copalyl diphosphate synthase feeding the syn-labdane branch.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar expression across leaves, fruit and trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: not trichome-enriched; function tested in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely functions in primary gibberellin metabolism rather than specialized bioactive diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient co-expression with EpTPS7 in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: modifying this gene could affect gibberellin homeostasis and growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in experiments but evidence indicates it is not a core specialized diterpenoid pathway gene for the medicinal metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited value for engineering the target medicinal diterpenoids; caution due to likely role in hormone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS4 clustered with ent-kaurene synthases involved in gibberellin biosynthesis. 2. It did not couple with Vitex specialized class II diTPSs but produced ent-kaurene with EpTPS7. 3. Expression pattern and activity indicate a role in primary metabolism rather than the focal medicinal diterpenoid pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes GGPP to kolavenyl diphosphate, precursor of clerodane diterpenes such as kolavelool after class I conversion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes relative to whole leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex trichomes; function tested in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: clerodane diterpenoids may contribute to dopaminergic activity in Vitex extracts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: provides kolavenyl diphosphate precursor for clerodane diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; coupling with SsSS; GC-MS; isolation of kolavenol derivative; NMR/HSQC; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: lack of strongly coupled native class I partner identified in this study may limit direct engineering outcomes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Weak dextrorotation observed for kolavenol derivative; lack of clear coupling with tested native class I enzymes suggests additional uncharacterized diTPSs likely exist in Vitex
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful starting enzyme for reconstructing clerodane diterpenoid pathways and exploring dopaminergic diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS5 was identified as a trichome-enriched class II diTPS. 2. Its product profile matched TwTPS14, and coupling with SsSS produced kolavelool. 3. VacTPS5 establishes a clerodane precursor branch in Vitex, although its native downstream class I partner was not clearly identified.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: labda-13(16),14-dien-9-ol from peregrinol diphosphate; synisopimara-7,15-diene and dehydroabietadiene from syn-CPP; 13R-(+)-manoyl oxide from (+)-8-LPP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts multiple diterpene diphosphate precursors into distinct diterpene hydrocarbons/alcohols including labda-13(16),14-dien-9-ol and abietane-related products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase, TPS-e/f, bi-domain enzyme lacking N-terminal gamma domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes relative to whole leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex trichomes; function tested in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to medicinal diterpenoid diversity in Vitex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: creates additional diterpene scaffolds from multiple precursors, expanding chemical diversity of Vitex bioactive diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient co-expression with class II diTPSs in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR; comparison with characterized references; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: substrate promiscuity may cause undesired by-products in engineered systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: At least one direct product from syn-CPP remains unresolved because dehydroabietadiene is likely a spontaneous oxidation product rather than the immediate enzymatic product; did not couple with VacTPS5
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable promiscuous class I diTPS for combinatorial biosynthesis of diverse diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacTPS6 is a trichome-enriched class I diTPS with broad substrate scope. 2. With VacTPS1 it forms labda-13(16),14-dien-9-ol, and with VacTPS3 it yields synisopimara-7,15-diene plus dehydroabietadiene-related products. 3. VacTPS6 substantially expands diterpene scaffold diversity and is a useful synthetic biology tool despite unresolved direct products in one branch.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: post-backbone oxidation of C-9 hydroxylated labdane diterpenes toward furan/lactone products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes 16-hydroxylation of peregrinol to form labd-13Z-ene-9,15,16-triol
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450, CYP76 family, CYP76BK subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaf and fruit trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in Vitex trichomes; function validated in yeast microsomes and engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae cultures
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan- and lactone-containing bioactive diterpenoids including vitexilactone-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dopaminergic medicinal diterpenoids linked to prolactin-lowering activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular trichomes on leaves and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: introduces a key oxidation step from peregrinol toward putative furan/lactone bioactive diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: trichome expression analysis by qRT-PCR; yeast microsomal in vitro assay; LC-HRMS; engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae in vivo production; NMR of isolated product
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CPR2 from poplar; CfCPR; VacTPS1; SsSS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction for yeast expression in microsome assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: leaf and trichome RNA-seq using Illumina HiSeq2000; de novo Trinity assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: native in planta function remains inferred rather than directly proven; additional oxidation enzymes are still needed for full target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First characterized member of the CYP76BK subfamily; no activity detected on 9,13(R)-epoxy-labd-14-ene, viteagnusin D or labda-13(16),14-dien-9-ol under tested conditions; product 17 was detected in Vitex trichome extracts, supporting pathway relevance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important oxidative module for reconstituting medicinal furan/lactone diterpenoid pathways in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacCYP76BK1 was identified as a trichome-enriched CYP76 family member related to diterpenoid-oxidizing CYPs. 2. Yeast microsomes and engineered yeast demonstrated that it hydroxylates peregrinol at C-16 to produce labd-13Z-ene-9,15,16-triol. 3. This reaction provides a plausible intermediate step toward furan- and lactone-containing bioactive diterpenoids in Vitex.
